title: p5.js Cheat Sheet
description: Essential p5.js commands and patterns for creative coding.

commands.description: Sets up the canvas with a width and height of 400 pixels.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to initialize the drawing canvas for your sketch.

commands.description: Continuously executes the code within it, clearing the canvas each frame.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to refresh the canvas with a background color every frame.

commands.description: Draws an ellipse at position (50, 50) with width and height of 80.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to draw a basic ellipse shape on the canvas.

commands.description: Draws a rectangle at position (30, 20) with width and height of 55.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to draw a rectangle on the canvas.
Use when you need to draw a straight line between two points.

commands.description: Sets the color used to fill shapes to red.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to change the fill color for shapes.

commands.description: Sets the color used to draw lines and borders to blue.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to change the stroke color for shapes.

commands.description: Stops the draw function from continuously executing.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to stop the draw loop for static sketches.

commands.description: Logs a message to the console when the mouse is pressed.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to trigger actions based on mouse input.

commands.description: Logs a message to the console when a key is pressed.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to trigger actions based on keyboard input.

commands.description: Loads an image and displays it at the top-left corner of the canvas.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to display images in your sketch.

commands.command: let myFont; function preload() { myFont = loadFont('assets/inconsolata.otf'); }
commands.description: Preloads a font for use in the sketch.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to load custom fonts before the sketch starts.

commands.description: Displays text at the specified coordinates.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to display text on the canvas.

commands.description: Saves the current canvas as a JPG file.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to export your canvas as an image file.
